## [00:00 - 03:01]

Hey, this is D-Pesh and this is the second part of the content planning matrix. We're going to take you through the 5W avatar. The 5W avatar is probably the central part of creating ads and creating funnels that are going to convert and work. It's such a simple document, but it brings together the core of what we're selling and why someone would be interested by breaking down the functional and the emotional. The basic structure and the 5Ws are what is the product? This is to the point, simple description, no fluff. Who am I actually selling to? Now, given any kind of business, you may have more than one avatar. If you're selling a watch, for example, one avatar could be men that are 25 to 45 and love fast cars. For example, the second avatar could be women who are also 25 to 45 and also love fast cars. They're two different avatars that may have different emotional and functional benefits. Chances are the functional benefits will be exactly the same, but the emotional may differ. That's really where I try to break the avatars out. For example, you may have another demographic for the same watch, but it's actually older men 55 plus who are retired, who have lots of income, but they see themselves as young and fun, for example. There's different ways you can break this avatar out. What I would do is really just put the simple descriptor here. Who, men 55 plus who have expendable income or whatever it is. Just keep these first two fields simple. I'll show you an example shortly. These two are the most important parts here and followed by the why not. The wow is what's the thing that's actually going to appeal to me and cut through my barriers. When people are on social media, they're not generally there to throw money at you, but many people will still buy. How do you actually cut through is by appealing to something which they find irresistible. A lot of these ads that go viral and really catch the imagination of people, they become irrational in liking the post and wanting to see the product as well. Here I list the benefits which cannot be measured. Something you can think, something you can emotionally feel, you can play on their ego. How do you make them feel better or look better? It could be a state change. We're going to elevate your state, your feeling, your consciousness. A pain and a solution. This is one of the core things around coaching and courses. For example, this is a lot of it is about pain and solution. When you're selling physical products, maybe there's not a pain, but maybe there's a state

change. For example, if you're selling cool outdoor gear, for example, the state change is that when you put this jacket on, you're going to look good, you're going to feel good, you can have a great time. If anyone had come to you and said, you know, I've got this sugar water, I want to sell it online, where would you even start? Coke have developed this over the years, but they have many, many emotional hooks. Having a good time, socializing with friends, etc., etc. If you look at their ads, even the Christmas ones with the holidays are coming and you've got the Santa truck, there's something about family and being together and stuff. They're always playing on emotions. The functional benefits, they're the most obvious things. You should list these out. Shipping time, product feel, the fit of the item, comfort, any money back, whether you're selling your physical product, a digital service, etc. It's all the list of functional benefits of your product, your program, or your brand. I guarantee that if you actually list this out, you would find yourself mostly leaning towards these when you're writing your copy, you're creating your ads. I want you to shift over to here. Really each line here on the left-hand side becomes a different angle for you to test. I don't recommend you throw everything into a single ad because you will overwhelm that person. Focus on a single emotional or intangible benefit. You can have some functional benefits as well, but they become your hooks. Then the why nots are so important because they are your objections and the reasons not to buy. We can talk about why they would buy it. They don't believe that your solution is right for them, etc. You need to list these ones out as well because they play a big part in being able to cut through the noise in the newsfeed. amazing and the most persuasive piece of copy you've ever written. If those first two lines let you down, then the rest of it's irrelevant because they're probably not going to scan down.
